Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to analyze two POWL models for a Hire to Retire process The standard process Post job ad screen candidates conduct interviews make hiring decision onboard employee add to payroll close case Typically screening happens after posting interviews after screening decision after interviews onboarding after decision payroll after onboarding close after payroll

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now examine model1 It is a strict partial order with edges Post to Screen Screen to Decide Screen to Interview Decide to Onboard Onboard to Payroll Payroll to Close Note There is no edge between Interview and Decide So after Screen both Decide and Interview can happen There is no ordering between Interview and Decide

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That is an anomaly The decision should come after interviews Also there is no direct edge from Interview to Decide so the process allows decision without interviews That is a severe anomaly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: A trace could be Post Screen Decide Interview Onboard which is wrong Or Post Screen Interview Decide which is correct if we assume they can be in any order However the model does not enforce interviews before decision So that is a flaw

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model2 It has a loop and XOR After Post both Screen and Interview can happen because there is Post to Screen and Post to Interview There is no order between them In standard process you screen before interview

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That means you could interview before screening That is an anomaly Also there is no edge from Screen to anything else Screen is only connected from Post That means Screen is an activity that can happen anytime after Post but it is not connected to the rest

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: There is no edge from Screen to Interview or Decide So the model allows that Screen happens but the rest of the process Interview Decide and so on can happen independently

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Screen could be executed after Post but also after Interview Since there are no constraints the ordering between Screen and Interview is not constrained The problem is that Screen is not a prerequisite for Interview

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The loop onboarding means Onboard can be repeated any number of times So you can have multiple onboarding events That is anomalous Usually you onboard once

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then xor payroll means either you add to payroll or skip it So payroll is optional That is a major anomaly Adding to payroll is a critical step after onboarding skipping it would be a severe error

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In model2 Screen has no relation to Close so you could do Post then Interview Decide loop xor Close and then finally Screen That means Screen could be the last activity which is odd because screening should happen before interviews and decision

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model2 has multiple severe anomalies screen not integrated payroll optional onboarding repeatable Model1 has one missing order that allows decision before interview

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model1 at least has all activities present and a sequential flow except for that In model2 some steps become optional and screen becomes isolated Which is more aligned with normative Model1 at least has all steps present and the rest of the flow is correct

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In model1 the correct order is possible because Interview can occur before Decide even though it is not enforced In model2 the incorrect order Interview before Screen is allowed and payroll can be skipped So model2 has more deviations
